game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Very Little Nightmares:** Very Little Nightmares is an isometric 2.5D puzzle adventure game where players guide the Girl in the Yellow Raincoat through a hostile mansion called "The Nest." Players control the girl by tapping on the screen to direct her movement to specific locations and tapping on interactive objects like levers, crates, or climbable surfaces to manipulate the environment. Progression is linear, with each room presenting environmental puzzles that require players to observe surroundings, utilize physics-based interactions (e.g., pushing a block onto a pressure plate), or find specific items to unlock new pathways. Stealth mechanics are crucial, demanding players to time movements and hide from enemies with defined patrol routes and detection cones, as being caught or falling into one of the numerous traps (e.g., spikes, bottomless pits) results in an immediate restart from the last checkpoint. The core objective is to solve all puzzles and evade all threats to escape the mansion and advance the overarching narrative.
game_info.description: Guide a small child through a dangerous mansion filled with deadly traps, patrolling enemies, and challenging puzzles. Navigate through rooms by moving carefully, pushing crates to create paths, avoiding spike traps, and hiding from enemies with vision cones. Solve environmental puzzles to unlock doors and progress through increasingly difficult challenges. One mistake means restarting the current room. Escape all rooms to win!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Move the character left, right, up (climb/interact), down (crouch/hide)
Space: Push/Pull crates when adjacent
Shift: Sprint (makes noise, attracts enemies)
Z: Interact with levers and objects
ESC: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart to title screen
Enter: Start game from title screen

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic movement and navigation through the first room without enemy interaction. Validates that the player can move in all directions and reach the exit door.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Move the player character using arrow keys to explore the room boundaries. Navigate to the exit door location and verify position updates are working correctly.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Player successfully moves around the room, position changes are reflected in gameState, and the character can reach different locations without errors. Movement mechanics work as intended.

TEST_2.test_description: Full playthrough to win the game. Tests optimal puzzle solving, crate manipulation, enemy avoidance, and progression through all rooms to achieve GAME_OVER_WIN state.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Execute optimal path through each room: push crates to solve puzzles, wait for enemy patrol patterns, use hiding spots when necessary, interact with levers, and progress through all rooms efficiently.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Player completes all rooms, solves all puzzles, avoids all enemies and traps, reaches the final exit, and triggers GAME_OVER_WIN condition. Game state reflects successful completion.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing enemy detection and stealth mechanics. Validates that enemies properly detect the player within their vision cones and patrol routes work correctly.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Deliberately enter enemy vision cones to test detection. Also test hiding mechanics and verify that staying out of vision prevents detection.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Enemies detect player when in vision cone, causing game over. Hiding mechanics work to prevent detection. Enemy patrol patterns function as designed.
TEST_3.description: Testing enemy detection and stealth mechanics. Validates that enemies properly detect the player within their vision cones and patrol routes work correctly.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing trap interactions and death conditions. Validates that spike traps and pits correctly trigger game over when touched.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Navigate player into spike traps and pit boundaries intentionally to verify collision detection and death mechanics work properly.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Contact with traps triggers room restart. Collision detection works accurately. Death and respawn mechanics function correctly.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing crate pushing mechanics and physics. Validates that crates can be pushed onto pressure plates and positioned correctly for puzzle solving.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Use Space key to push crates in different directions. Position crates on pressure plates and verify they activate switches and unlock doors.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Crates move when pushed, stay in position when released, activate pressure plates when positioned correctly, and enable puzzle progression.
